Consumers are taking charge of their health using digital tools
- 4 million downloads per health app

A Comparison of Healthcare Providers by Digital Profile:

Digitally Mature Healthcare Providers:
- 58% use social media to offer services to customers 
- 47% use mobile channels
- 78% have a common vision across senior management for digital transformation
- 84% allocate adequate funding
- 63% have automated core processes
- 94% have an integrated view of data
- 84% are building digital skills

Non-Digitally Mature Healthcare Providers:
- 18% use social media to offer services
- 18% use mobile channels
- 28% have a common vision across senior management
- 31% allocate adequate funding
- 31% have automated core processes
- 33% have an integrated view of data
- 38% are building digital skills

How Can Healthcare Providers Move Up the Digital Curve?
1. Define a Vision and Scope
- Focus on patient needs
- Assess existing initiatives
- Build digital roadmap

2. Establish a Transformation Roadmap and Governance Model to Drive Digital Initiatives
- Identify quick wins and long-term solutions
- Set up dedicated Digital Services Unit (DSU)

3. Promote Internal Collaboration and Knowledge Sharing to Drive Internal Engagement
- Build digital communication backbone
- Leverage internal champions of digital
- Hire experienced digital practitioners

4. Prioritize Skill Development and Operational Excellence for a Sustained Digital Advantage
- Invest in training programs
- Streamline operations
- Single source of truth for customer solutions

Although gun violence in America is frequently attributed to the availability of firearms, another relevant factor is our nation's broken mental health care system. Thus, if we hope to curb gun violence, we must de-stigmatize and treat mental health disorders.

GUN VIOLENCE IN AMERICA

2/3 OF ALL MASS KILLINGS INVOLVE GUNS.

ACCESS TO GUNS

FEDERAL GUN LAWS CURRENTLY ENACTED INCLUDE:

- Prohibition of firearm sales or transfers to those suffering from mental health disorders
- Prohibition of firearm sales to those who have been involuntarily committed to a mental institution
- Prohibition of firearm sales to persons under indictment or convicted of felonies
- Prohibition of firearm sales to individuals prohibited by federal law
- Prohibition of private sales by unlicensed sellers without background checks
- Prohibition of certain firearm purchases by other restricted categories

Gun control laws are further weakened by discrepancies in regulations for licensed firearm dealers, which are stricter, and regulations for private sales, which are more lax.

TAPPING THE MARKET
Expanding Access to Sanitation for the Poor Through the Domestic Private Sector

2.5 BILLION People across the developing world use shared or unimproved sanitation facilities

- $1.2 BILLION

- $0.206 BILLION

What is the average cost of latrine/toilet pumping as a percentage of average monthly household income?
- 39%

- 48%

What percent of enterprises identify the poor as target customers?
- 62%
- 66%
- N/A
- 48%

WHAT ARE THE FACTORS DRIVING DEMAND?
[Listed by country]
Bangladesh: GDP Growth, Poverty Decline
Indonesia: GDP Growth, Poverty Decline, Sanitation Marketing
Peru: GDP Growth, Poverty Decline, Private Sector Interest in Sanitation Promotion
Tanzania: GDP Growth, Poverty Decline, Sanitation Marketing, Household Subsidies for the Poor, Policy Supporting Public-Private Partnerships

WHAT ARE CONSTRAINTS TO INVESTMENT?
- Unmet Customer Needs
- Weak Supply Chain  
- Difficult Investment Climate

RECOMMENDATIONS:
1. Stimulate Demand
- Show consumers that improved sanitation is a smart economic decision
- Design sanitation solutions that match consumer preferences, needs and preferences
- Offer flexible financing options

2. Strengthen the Sanitation Supply Chain
- Build business capacity and entrepreneurship
- Encourage formation of strategic partnerships to develop stronger distribution channels to reach remote customers

3. Improve the Investment Climate
- Communicate clear policies that support private sector provision
- Facilitate private sector provision by clearly defining the role of the private sector in government strategy and policy

MARKET DRIVERS

WHAT ARE CONSTRAINTS TO INVESTMENT?

UNMET CUSTOMER NEEDS
"Why should I pay for a toilet or latrine?"
"Why should I settle if I can't afford the dream?"

The poor are challenging customers who would rather make do with their current sanitation situation if they cannot find a desirable, affordable product in their local market.

WEAK SUPPLY CHAIN
"How do I reach poor households?"
"Why should I prioritize sanitation in my portfolio?"

The sanitation market is highly fragmented and uncoordinated, making it difficult to develop and deliver products that consumers are willing and able to buy. Enterprises with the scale to tackle these industry challenges do not currently see sanitation or the poor as priority markets; businesses that do focus on sanitation have limited reach and product differentiation.

DIFFICULT INVESTMENT CLIMATE
"How can we improve the ease of doing business in the sanitation sector?"
"Why should we invest in this even riskier business?"

Current sanitation policies are not focused on lifting barriers to market entry. Despite the existence of government sanitation promotion programs, sanitation enterprises do not have enough information about viable markets and lack sufficient access to finance.

KEY CHALLENGES

1
Stimulate Demand
- Show consumers that good sanitation is a smart economic decision
- Design sanitation solutions that meet poor customers' needs and preferences
- Offer flexible financing options

2
Strengthen the Sanitation Supply Chain
- Build business capacity and market intelligence
- Encourage formation of strategic partnerships to develop stronger distribution channels to reach remote customers

3
Improve the Investment Climate
- Communicate clear policies that promote improved sanitation
- Facilitate private sector provision by clearly defining the role of the private sector in government strategy and policy

RECOMMENDATIONS

MARKET DRIVERS

WHAT ARE CONSTRAINTS TO INVESTMENT?

UNMET CUSTOMER NEEDS

"Why should I pay for a toilet or latrine?"
"Why should I settle if I can't afford the dream?"

The poor are challenging customers who would rather make do with their current sanitation situation if they cannot find a desirable, affordable product in their local market.

WEAK SUPPLY CHAIN

"How do I reach poor households?"
"Why should I prioritize sanitation in my portfolio?"

The sanitation market is highly fragmented and uncoordinated, making it difficult to develop and deliver products that consumers are willing and able to buy. Enterprises with the scale to tackle these industry challenges do not currently see sanitation or the poor as priority markets; businesses that do focus on sanitation have limited reach and product differentiation.

DIFFICULT INVESTMENT CLIMATE

"How can we improve the ease of doing business in the sanitation sector?"
"Why should we invest in this even riskier business?"

Current sanitation policies are not focused on lifting barriers to market entry. Despite the existence of government sanitation promotion programs, sanitation enterprises do not have enough information about viable markets and lack sufficient access to finance.

KEY CHALLENGES

RECOMMENDATIONS

1 Stimulate Demand
- Show consumers that good sanitation is a smart economic decision
- Design sanitation solutions that meet poor customers' needs and preferences
- Offer flexible financing options

2 Strengthen the Sanitation Supply Chain
- Build business capacity and market intelligence
- Encourage formation of strategic partnerships to develop stronger distribution channels to reach remote customers

3 Improve the Investment Climate
- Communicate clear policies that promote improved sanitation
- Facilitate private sector provision by clearly defining the role of the private sector in government strategy and policy

TOP 10 MOST EXPENSIVE CITIES TO LIVE IN 2010

1. LUANDA
Angola
Prices for housing and food continue to rise dramatically in Luanda, making this city the most expensive place to live in for 2010.

The city has experienced an oil boom, which has meant Luanda grew well beyond its means, very quickly, and attracted waves of foreign workers - all competing for the now scarce resources.

When we estimated the two thirds of Angolans live on less than $2 a day, most will never be able to afford a car or even their kitchen, even though the oil boom has made gasoline the most affordable commodity where one litre of gas costs just $0.55. To rent a luxury two bedroom apartment will cost $7,000 per month and even a fast food meal will cost you $17.50.

2. TOKYO
Japan
Tokyo is one of the most populated cities in the world, and as the capital of Japan, Tokyo now has more than 13 million people living in the 2,000 square kilometres of the city. As a result, housing in a penthouse in central Tokyo is often hit by earthquakes, and can be costly to maintain because of insurance. It also boasts some of the world's most expensive land is Tokyo.

Expats often need to live in the city, and as a result can find themselves paying $4,436 a month for a luxury two bedroom apartment.

3. N'DJAMENA
Chad
N'Djamena has a high cost of living due to years of civil war, which has made foreign workers reluctant to invest any capital into the region. The infrastructure and local markets have been ravaged by the civil unrest and food is in short supply. The remoteness of the region and high transport costs have helped make prices high for those products which can be brought in. In the end services are expensive, with the average cost of the most basic items being higher than in the world's wealthy regions such as Geneva and Tokyo. In N'Djamena one of the poorest in the world also, where 80% of the population live below the poverty line.

4. MOSCOW
Russia
In Moscow, the energy, financial, software development and engineering industries attract a large and wealthy expatriate community. This means that prices for real estate, retail and services have been pushed significantly higher.

This new found wealth has meant that salaries have also inflated many luxury retailers. Moscow has seen an increase in luxury hotels and restaurants, but even a $3,600 per month rent on a luxury 2 bed apartment is not uncommon. In the centre of Moscow, a coffee can cost $8.29 making this the most expensive city for a cup of coffee in this list.

5. GENEVA
Switzerland
Geneva is a truly global city, the head of the United Nations and the Red Cross, and home to numerous financial institutions. Geneva is a global city and the centre for diplomacy and the international community. In Geneva food prices are 45% more expensive than anywhere else in western Europe and Switzerland also has some of the world's most expensive rent and apartments in Geneva are expensive as well. The city is home to the global elite and as such costs reflect this. A two bedroom luxury apartment starting at $4,010 per month.

6. OSAKA
Japan
On the same island as Tokyo, Osaka also has a high population density of approximately 2 million people. There are also a number of large manufacturers, and the local economy is built around heavy industry and transport. The central business district is also a major retail and business hub and as a result, rents and items for everyday use such as coffee are expensive.

7. LIBREVILLE
Gabon
The capital, Libreville lies in a coastal city and as a result is the country's largest city, and a major port and trade centre for lumber. The desire for expatriates to continue to consume western foods and goods makes Libreville very expensive to live in. Many of the items and goods for local consumption are imported and as a result the cost of living is high. Fast food meals can be expensive and local groceries and fresh foods are costly. For most of us, will cost $27.08.

8. HONG KONG
Tied in 8th place, Hong Kong is an expensive hub of trade and finance with a highly developed infrastructure and services and particularly real estate, where housing is at a premium. Hong Kong has the highest population density of any major city and as such space is at a premium. With 1,104 square kilometres, and is home to more than 6.8 million people, living in Hong Kong is expensive. With $4,248 per month, a luxury two bedroom apartment in Hong Kong will cost around $4,248 per month.

9. ZURICH
Switzerland
Zurich is the commercial capital of Switzerland - fulfilling its role as a major business centre, and the headquarters for many foreign corporations. Zurich is the most expensive city in Switzerland and this attracts some of the top minds from around the world.

Zurich has the largest number of company bankruptcies in 2009 according to corelation agency Dun & Bradstreet. The inflation rate has also been increasing for 2010 after a dip the previous year, and the city has a very strong economy.

10. COPENHAGEN
Denmark
Copenhagen is an expensive place to live due to the strong wage rates and high taxation. The city's high quality of life, with state welfare and worker protections, combined with high prices for goods and services make Copenhagen expensive. Quality of life in Copenhagen is one of the highest in the world, which reflects its high cost of living.

Copenhagen is home to the world's largest wind turbine manufacturer, producing much of the world's wind turbines. The cost of a luxury two bedroom apartment in Copenhagen is $1,94.

#globalfinindex
GLOBAL FINDEX 2014
Financial Inclusion

Financial inclusion is critical in reducing poverty and achieving inclusive economic growth. When people can participate in the financial system, they are better able to save, borrow and invest to improve their lives. In 2011 the World Bank launched the Global Findex database, the world’s most comprehensive set of data on how people save, borrow, make payments, and manage risk. The updated 2014 Global Findex database shows the strongest growth in account ownership and use of financial services, and presents opportunities to expand it further.

There has been significant growth in financial inclusion

More adults have an account (with a financial institution or mobile money service) now than three years ago.

THE POTENTIAL OF SOCIAL MEDIA FOR B2B IN 2016

Social Media

Social media is not going away; it's up to businesses to board the train or get left on the platform.
What other tool can build relationships between businesses with such ease and speed?
What other channel can generate enhanced exposure for your business and develop brand loyalty without you spending a penny?
In 2016, B2B social media will generate leads for over half of all businesses and continue to break down barriers between you and those you wish to connect with... if you let it.

BENEFITS TO B2B BUSINESSES

Reduced Marketing Expenses 50%
Improved Sales 51%
Grow Business Partnerships 55%
Established Thought Leadership 55%
Improved Search Rankings 58%
Generated Leads 65%
Provided Marketplace Insight 68%
Developed Loyal Fans 69%
Increased Traffic 77%
Increased Exposure 90%

54%
of B2B marketers are generating lead from social media

The sheer reach of social media continues to be its most indisputable attribute. Businesses can now interact with their target market through a multitude of platforms on the go 24/7, whether they're in your store or commuting to work on the train.

90%
of enterprises say that they respond to customer service enquiries on social media

72%
When a brand is followed on Twitter, 72% of the followers are likely to make a future purchase from that brand

TOP SOCIAL MEDIA SITES

Facebook 1.59 billion users each month
YouTube 1.4 billion users each month
Instagram 400 million active monthly users
Twitter 320 million active monthly users
Pinterest 100 million active monthly users
LinkedIn 400 million users but only 25% use it monthly

Above are the monthly active users for the most popular social networks around. Instagram was the big winner of 2015, doubling its active users in the space of 12 months to 400m users. No other social network - not even Facebook - has reached 400m users in five years' existence. It took Facebook six years to reach that level. This highlights the role that imagery has to play; like the old saying goes, it can still say a thousand words. And say it instantly.

SOCIAL MEDIA ADVERT SPEND

75%
More than a third of CMOs say that digital marketing will account for 75% or more of their spending within the next five years

51%
51% of B2B marketers used paid ads on social media as part of their marketing strategy in 2015

16%
In the next 12 months, B2B service companies will spend 16% of their marketing budget on social media

16%
By 2017, social network ad spending will reach $35.98 billion, representing 16.0% of all digital ad spending globally

CONTENT USED

Video
YouTube is the most highly engaged website of all the social media platforms

Infographics
Infographics are liked and shared three times more than other content and users are 30 times more likely to read them than text

Images
Having at least one image in your blog post results in 94% more Facebook shares and 2.36x more Twitter shares

KEY CHALLENGES

17%
17% of B2B service companies have not been able to show the impact of social media on their business

30%
Only 30% of marketing projects run by B2B service companies use marketing analytics

15%

BUSINESS ANALYTICS vs DATA SCIENCE

In the age of big data, parsing unwieldy amounts of information can lead to world-changing innovation.
To understand this data, companies are hiring a variety of specialists, including business analysts and data scientists.

Who Are They?

BUSINESS ANALYSTS
Research and extract valuable information from structured and unstructured sources to explain historical, current, and future business performance; determine the best analytical models and approaches to present and explain solutions to business users.

DATA SCIENTISTS
Design, develop, and deploy algorithms through statistical programming that support business decision-making tools; manage large amounts of data; create visualizations to aid in understanding.

What Education Do They Have?

The majority of business analysts come from a variety of backgrounds including business and humanities, while data scientists tend to come from computer science, mathematics, and technology backgrounds.

BUSINESS ANALYSTS
30% Bachelor's
56% Master's
11% Doctoral

DATA SCIENTISTS
28% Bachelor's
48% Master's
20% Doctoral

Sample Undergraduate Majors

Business Analysts
• Business Administration
• Information Technology
• Finance
• Political Science
• Anthropology
• Economics
• History
• Psychology

Data Scientists
• Mathematics
• Computer Science
• Information Science
• Computer Software Engineering
• Technical Communications
• Computer Information Systems
• Statistics

What Skills Do They Have?

Both business analysts and data scientists are experts in using data to inform decisions, but they apply their skills in different ways, using the same or similar tools. The skills listed below describe someone who has earned a master's degree in the field.

Decision-Making
Business Analysts
• Instigate change by transforming business insights from interdisciplinary data analysis into tangible resources for business decision-making.

Data Scientists
• Discover the opportunity in datasets by mining data and writing machine learning algorithms to support decision-making processes.

Applied Problem Solving
Business Analysts
• Define business problems and translate statistical analysis into data-driven business intelligence that improves business performance.

Data Scientists
• Create the foundation of analysis by which an applied business problem can be solved.

Data Analysis
Business Analysts
• Research, interpret, and visualize raw data using predictive, prescriptive and descriptive analysis to make it useful and accessible for business users.

Data Scientists
• Manage and organize large sets of multivariate data using methods like linear discriminant analysis and multilinear regression selection.

Analytical Models
Business Analysts
• Understand, integrate, and prescribe solutions that use data modeling.

Data Scientists
• Experienced statistical programmers using languages and tools such as SAS, SQL, R, SPSS, Python, and Knime.

Database Management
Business Analysts
• Define and align database requirements for various forms of data, coded and not coded, using tools like Teradata, Oracle, and Hadoop.

Data Scientists
• Design and structure databases using tools like Teradata, Oracle, and Hadoop.

Where Do They Work?

BUSINESS ANALYSTS
25% Finance and insurance
10% Computer systems design and related services
8% Manufacturing
8% State and local government, excluding education and hospitals
8% Management of companies and enterprises

DATA SCIENTISTS
26% Federal government

13% Colleges, universities, and professional schools
11% Research and development in the physical, engineering, and life sciences
8% Software publishers

WOMEN AND CRIMINAL VICTIMIZATION

Violence against women is an important issue that impacts individuals, families and society and which continues to be a barrier to gender equality in Canada and around the world.

1. OVER 1.2 MILLION VIOLENT VICTIMIZATION INCIDENTS
2014 | self-reported* by women
Violent victimizations include sexual assault, robbery and physical assault.

2. 2015 | 88.2% OF SEXUAL ASSAULT VICTIMS ARE FEMALE

3. RISK FACTORS
Violence can affect anyone, but some factors can put individuals at higher risk of being victimized.

4. YOUNG WOMEN
aged 15 to 24
Violent victimization was the highest among young women in this age category, with 215 incidents per 1,000 population.

RATES OF VICTIMIZATION DECLINE WITH AGE
*per 1,000 population
aged 25 to 34: 128 incidents
aged 35 to 44: 68 incidents*
aged 45 to 54: 68 incidents
aged 55 to 64: 42 incidents*
aged 64 and older: 14 incidents

5. INDIGENOUS WOMEN
experienced violence at a rate 2.7 times higher than non-Indigenous women in 2014.

1980: 9% of all female homicide victims were Indigenous
2015: 24% of all female homicide victims were Indigenous

6. LESBIAN AND BISEXUAL WOMEN
experienced violence at more than twice the rate reported by gay and bisexual men in 2014.

121 incidents* - Gay and bisexual men
281 incidents* - Lesbian and bisexual women
(*per 1,000 population)

*The two primary sources of data are police-reported crime data, and self-reported victimization. Self-reported incidents may not have come to the attention of the police and were collected through the General Social Survey on Canadians' safety.

question: which touch screen is more durable - resistive or infrared?
prediction_claude_sonnet__claude_sonnet: Based on the document text, infrared touchscreens are more durable than resistive touchscreens. The document shows that resistive screens have "Fair Durability" and are vulnerable to scratches, with screens wearing over time and needing replacement. In contrast, infrared screens have "Good Durability" with glass that only shatters upon major impact.
extracted_text_claude_sonnet__claude_son:
HOW DOES A TOUCHSCREEN PHONE WORK?
TECHNOLOGY

There are three types of technologies widely used in touch screen phones today: resistive, capacitive, and infrared.

RESISTIVE
- Phones: Touch Diamond, LG Dare
- How it works: When the top flexible layer is pressed into the lower layer, the electrical current changes and the location of impact is calculated
- Cost to make: $
- Screen material: Layer of flexible material (usually polyester film) over a layer of glass
- Tool for input: Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus
- Outdoor visibility: Poor visibility
- Multi-touch capabilities?: No
- Durability: Fair Durability, screens are vulnerable to scratches and slow screen will wear over time and need replacing

CAPACITIVE
- Phones: Samsung Messenger Touch, Samsung Instinct, HTC BlackBerry Storm
- How it works: The current is broadcast from the corners of the screen. When a finger touches the screen, a grid of invisible beams just above the screen breaks
- Cost to make: $$
- Screen material: Glass
- Tool for input: Bare fingers only
- Outdoor visibility: Good visibility
- Multi-touch capabilities?: Yes
- Durability: Good Durability, glass shatters upon major impact only

INFRARED (HEAT AND OPTICAL)
- Phones: Samsung U600 (heat), Neonode N2 (optical)
- How it works: Heat-sensitive must be touched with a warm object (like fingers) point of heat is calculated
- Cost to make: $$$
- Screen material: Glass
- Tool for input: Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus

SOCIAL MEDIA MARKETING BY THE NUMBERS

Statistics:
- 22 million views in one week during McDonalds campaign
- 7 million views of Spice's "Moving Band"
- 86.5 million number of impressions for Coca-Cola's "Retweeted Tweet" in June 2010
- 750 million Facebook users
- 33% increase during McDonald's campaign
- 70% percentage of Facebook users that visit the site daily
- 52% percentage of social networking done on Facebook
- 92% percentage of small business on Facebook say social media is important for marketing
- 18,000 number of clicks American Airlines gets per promotion tweet
- 65.5 million number of views in 2 years
- 20 million impressions generated by Network Solutions' Facebook Promoted Tweets
- 400 Number of checkins at Fatburger in 45 days
- 35,000 Number of tweets with the #LouisVuitton conference in January 2011
- 10,000 Number of YouTube brand partners
- 12 million Facebook Likes in a 3-month SCVNGR campaign at Buffalo Wild Wings
- $1.34 Average value driven by a Facebook Like, as compared to just 10 cents for a tweet
- 11% The percentage of people who say they would buy something that was offered to them on Facebook
- 4 billion The number of "things" shared on Facebook each day
- 500,000 Number of merchants on Foursquare
- 381,576,305 Number of Foursquare checkins in 2010

6 SOCIAL MEDIA CAMPAIGNS & THEIR METRICS

Campaign Details:
1. Client: Red Bull
Agency: Alpine + Kennedy
Campaign: "The Day Your Man Could Fly"
Results: +60% Facebook fans

2. Client: EA Games
Agency: Studio Toronto
Campaign: Used Facebook offered to publicize its upcoming Bulletstorm game
Results: +400% likes

3. Client: Bud Light
Agency: Digital Influence Group
Campaign: Created a Facebook App contest lasted about 5 weeks, from March 25-June 1
Results: +130% likes, 18,500 entries

4. Client: Universal
Agency: Small Like
Campaign: Undergrads applied to be the "Ultimate Intern" by submitting 140-character tweets
Results: +2700% Twitter followers, 150,000 YouTube subscribers

5. Client: Free The Children
Agency: Momentum Media
Campaign: Agency started a Facebook Page for "We Day." Users who "liked" the Page and register for a sample would get $1 donation matched by Canadian charity Free The Children
Results: 108,000 total votes cast, 800 wall posts, 3,500 likes

6. Client: Neil Widen Bohren
Agency: Merchantcircle
Campaign: The agency created a Facebook Page that invited users to try a trade-free sample, explaining why they should want to test the product and if they'd blog about it
Results: 69,500 views of bulletstorm content and demo videos, 5,000 users got samples

Sources: Mashable, Facebook Studio, Pew Research, ZDNet

GLOBAL FINDEX 2014
Financial Inclusion

Financial inclusion is critical in reducing poverty and achieving inclusive economic growth. When people can participate in the financial system, they are better able to start and grow businesses, invest in their children’s education, and save for the future.
Global Findex database, the world’s most comprehensive set of data on how people save, pay, borrow, and manage risk. The updated 2014 Global Findex database shows great progress in expanding financial inclusion and creating opportunities to expand it further.

THERE HAS BEEN SIGNIFICANT GROWTH IN FINANCIAL INCLUSION

More adults have an account (with a financial institution or mobile money service) now than three years ago.

51% of the world’s adults had an account 2011
2.5 BILLION ADULTS WITHOUT AN ACCOUNT 2011
62% of the world’s adults have an account 2014

Examples include Mexico, Brazil, India, China, Tanzania, and Indonesia.
MEXICO 27% 2011 / 61% 2014
BRAZIL 34% 2011 / 68% 2014
INDIA 53% 2011 / 59% 2014
CHINA 64% 2011 / 79% 2014
TANZANIA 40% 2011 / 2014
INDONESIA 20% 2011 / 36% 2014

Account ownership has increased in nearly every developing country.
41% 2011
54% 2014

STILL, THERE ARE OPPORTUNITIES TO EXPAND FINANCIAL INCLUSION, PARTICULARLY AMONG WOMEN AND THE POOR

Globally, the gender gap remains unchanged. And the gender gap in developing countries remains a steady percentage point.
59% of men have an account 2014
50% of women have an account 2014
46% of men have an account 2011
37% of women have an account 2011

REGIONALLY, THE GENDER GAP IN ACCOUNT OWNERSHIP REMAINS LARGEST IN SOUTH ASIA
SOUTH ASIA
MEN 55%
WOMEN 37%

AMONG THE WORLD’S POOR, FINANCIAL INCLUSION IS IMPROVING, BUT NOT ENOUGH.
POOREST 17%?
RICHEST 60% OF HOUSEHOLDS IN DEVELOPING COUNTRIES HAVE AN ACCOUNT
60%

IN SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA, MOBILE MONEY ACCOUNT OWNERSHIP IS DRIVING A HUGE EXPANSION OF FINANCIAL INCLUSION
6 MILLION EAST ASIA & PACIFIC
30 MILLION SOUTH ASIA
64 MILLION SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
In 5 of these 13 countries, more adults now have a mobile money account than an account at a financial institution.

By shifting transfers and wages digitally into accounts, governments can increase the number of adults who have an account by up to 160 MILLION.
By paying private sector wages digitally into accounts, companies can increase the number of adults with an account by up to 280 MILLION.

AMERICA'S GUN VIOLENCE EPIDEMIC

Although gun violence in America is frequently attributed to the availability of firearms, another relevant factor is our nation's broken mental health care system. Although most people who suffer from mental health disorders are nonviolent, severe mental health disorders that go untreated can increase the risk for violence. Thus, if we hope to curb gun violence, we must de-stigmatize and treat mental health disorders.

GUN VIOLENCE IN AMERICA

2/3 OF ALL MASS KILLINGS INVOLVE GUNS.

In America, a mass killing — defined as having 4 victims, not including the killer — occurs once every 2 weeks.

There were 154 mass killings from 2006 to 2010.

THOSE INCLUDE
774 VICTIMS

OF THOSE VICTIMS,
161 WERE CHILDREN

GUNS & SUICIDE

31,000 GUN RELATED DEATHS IN THE UNITED STATES

19,000 OF THOSE ARE SELF INFLICTED

5% OF ALL SUICIDE ATTEMPTS ARE BY GUNSHOT.

85% OF THOSE ATTEMPTS RESULTED IN DEATH.

Suicide is the 11th leading cause of death in the United States and the third-leading cause of death for people ages 10-24 years.

In July 2007, a nationwide report indicated that male veterans are twice as likely to die by suicide as compared to their civilian peers.

ACCESS TO GUNS
FEDERAL GUN LAWS CURRENTLY ENACTED INCLUDE:

- Prohibition of firearm sales or transfers to those suffering from mental health disorders and those adjudicated mentally ill.
- Prohibition of firearm possession in schools, unless licensed by that school or the firearm is unloaded and in a locked container.
- Federal background checks on firearms purchases from licensed dealers; records of which must legally be destroyed after 24 hours.
- Prohibition of possession of fully automatic weapons by civilians.
- Prohibition of children to possess a handgun or ammunition, with exceptions for employment, practice, education and home defense.

BUT GUN CONTROL IS LARGELY DICTATED BY INDIVIDUAL STATES, CREATING LARGE DISCREPANCIES BETWEEN STATES. FOR EXAMPLE, SOUTH DAKOTA HAS SOME OF THE WEAKEST GUN LAWS AND CALIFORNIA HAS SOME OF THE STRONGEST.

SOUTH DAKOTA
- No laws restricting the reporting of mental health information to the National Instant Criminal Background Check System.
- No civil commitment information required.
- No firearm registration.
- No magazine capacity restriction.
- Handgun open carry permitted and concealed carry allowed.

CALIFORNIA
- Only state to track and disarm violent offenders and those deemed a danger to themselves or others.
- State laws require universal background checks on all firearm transfers.
- Waiting periods for handgun purchases.
- Assault weapons ban in effect.
- Limitations on magazine capacity.
- Stronger mental health reporting requirements.

Gun control laws are further weakened by discrepancies in regulations for licensed firearms dealers, which are stricter, and regulations for private sales, which are more lax.

MENTAL HEALTH AND GUN VIOLENCE

7 MILLION
Americans suffer from severe mental health issues, such as schizophrenia, acute depression or bipolar disorder.

The increased risk of violence for most mentally ill people is minimal, but when paired with issues like substance abuse and life stressors, the risk is more pronounced.

HALF OF U.S. ADULTS WILL DEVELOP A MENTAL ILLNESS DURING THEIR LIFETIME.

People with severe mental illness are roughly twice as likely to commit violent acts in their lifetime.

THE FLOUNDERING MENTAL HEALTH SYSTEM

LESS THAN HALF OF AMERICANS WITH SEVERE MENTAL HEALTH ISSUES RECEIVE ADEQUATE TREATMENT.

$1.6 BILLION in state mental health services have been cut since 2009.

These cuts led to significant reductions in hospital and community services for those with serious mental illness, including cutbacks to:
HOUSING
OUTPATIENT INTERVENTIONS
MEDICATION
CRISIS SERVICES

13% of children ages 8 to 15 live with severe mental health disorders.

BUT ONLY 20% of those children receive treatment.

ONE-HALF OF ALL LIFETIME CASES OF MENTAL ILLNESS BEGIN BY AGE 14. YET THERE ARE LONG DELAYS — SOMETIMES DECADES — BETWEEN THE FIRST ONSET OF SYMPTOMS AND WHEN PEOPLE SEEK AND RECEIVE TREATMENT.

500,000 TOTAL BEDS AVAILABLE IN AMERICAN MENTAL INSTITUTIONS AT PEAK LEVELS IN THE 1950s

43,000 TOTAL BEDS CURRENTLY AVAILABLE

This shortage of inpatient psychiatric care makes finding appropriate care difficult, if not impossible.

IN 2011
24 STATES SLASHED MENTAL HEALTH PROVIDER REIMBURSEMENT RATES FOR MEDICAID — THE MOST IMPORTANT SOURCE OF FUNDING OF PUBLIC MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES.

REAL SOLUTIONS

“We must not only tackle gun control but we must also fix deficiencies in the U.S. mental health system, gaps that can leave many people with serious mental illness and family members without an easy way to get the treatment and support we know can help avert catastrophes.”

Lynn R. Goldman, pediatrician and dean of the George Washington University School of Public Health and Health Services

New Assault Weapons Ban
Senator Dianne Feinstein has authored the Assault Weapons Ban of 2013, which would ban the sale and transfer of semi-automatic assault weapons and large-capacity magazines.

Funding Mental Health Care
The recently passed Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act will expand Medicaid coverage to more Americans to access mental health services and require mental health care coverage as an essential health benefit.

Gun Violence Research
On March 1, President Obama proposed increasing funding for gun violence research to inform evidence-based policies and prevention measures.

Destigmatizing Mental Illness
On February 4, the Department of Health & Human Services launched a national public education campaign to encourage earlier detection and treatment of mental illness, reduce stigma, and connect people to care.

ON TANGIBILITY
Real world experiment with tangible/intangible charities

ON TANGIBILITY
Real world experiment with tangible/intangible projects

[Emoji scale showing progression from sad to happy faces]

SOME TIPS
- Create real tangible projects for donors to fund
- Encourage donors to share their donation (and amount) in public
- Be specific in how donations will be used to make an impact
- Build towards your goal before asking public for donations
- Share stories of other donors' generosity before asking

THE RECAP
- The tangible charity received almost three times as much
- Framed in a tangible way received almost twice as much
- Citing a prior donation of $300 increased giving an average of 29%
- Support increased when told how far from goal at 65-85% of the goal

REFERENCES
Science of giving: Experimental approaches to the study of charity

question: Which audio media has the least amount of lifespan with regular use?
prediction_claude_sonnet__claude_sonnet: According to the document, both Reel Audio Tape (1950s) and Cassette Tape (1963) have the same lowest lifespan range of 10-20 years among audio media formats. These two formats tie for the shortest lifespan in the audio media category.
extracted_text_claude_sonnet__claude_son:
THE LIFESPAN OF STORAGE MEDIA

For as long we have been inventing ways to capture and present data, we have been developing media on which to store it. With each new innovation, we've seen greater convenience, increased storage capacity, and longer lifespans. But at some point, even with the utmost care, media fails... leaving us with a useless piece of plastic, tape, or metal.

The Cloud, with its extensive network of server farms, leverages redundant storage media, ensuring that in the event of hardware failure, the data lives on, stored safely in another part of the cloud. By way of comparison, we've assembled some lifespan statistics for storage media throughout the digital ages.

Icons: FAST | REDUNDANT | RELIABLE | ACCESSIBLE ANYWHERE | FOREVER

COMPUTER MEDIA
- ZIP DISK (1994): 2-10 years
- 5" FLOPPY (1976): 2-30 years
- 3.5" FLOPPY DISK (1982): 2-15 years
- CD-RW (1997): 3-100 years
- DIGITAL TAPE (1987): 10-30 years
- USB FLASH DRIVE (2000): 10-75 years
- DVD-R (1999): 30-100 years
- HARD DRIVE (1996): 34-100 years
- SOLID STATE HARD DRIVE (1999): 51-100+ years

AUDIO MEDIA
- COMPACT DISC (1982): 3-100 years
- REEL AUDIO TAPE (1950s): 10-20 years
- CASSETTE TAPE (1963): 10-20 years
- EIGHT TRACK (1964): 10-30 years
- MINI DISC (1992): 15-50 years
- VINYL RECORD (1881-1890s): 50-100 years

VIDEO MEDIA
- VHS TAPE (1972): 5-15 years
- DV TAPE (1995): 10-20 years
- DVD (1995): 30-100 years
- SUPER 8 FILM (1965): 70-100 years

PHOTO MEDIA
- UNDEVELOPED FILM (1889): 3-6 years
- DIGITAL PHOTOS (1990s): 30-50 years
- PHOTO SLIDES (1961): 50-70 years
- DEVELOPED PHOTOS (1900s): 50-70 years
- MEMORY CARD (1999): 115 years

Notes:
- CLICK OF DEATH: When the read/write heads make a clicking sound suddenly click. That signified that the disk was no longer usable and all data is lost.
- LOST IN SPACE: USB drives are often lost long before they ever fail
- HEAD CRASH: When the media over writes into the disk cylinder and crashes the drive
- COMPATIBILITY: 8-Tracks became an odd format when players disappeared
- DJ SCRATCH-N-SNIFF: DJs purposely damage their vinyl records in the name of learning how to DJ
- BE KIND REWIND: VHS tapes would often tear or break due to constant rewinding
- VIDEO "HITS": Decaying digital tapes will drop frames and pixels during playback
- COLOR FADES: B&W Photos will last longer than color. The dyes in colors are less stable
- GRANDMASTER FLASH: What fails is to read and not from time

ADDITIONAL WAYS MEDIA CAN FAIL:
- COMPATIBILITY ISSUES
- OXIDATION/CHEMICAL BREAKDOWN
- DRY OUT
- PHYSICAL DAMAGE

[Source citations and copyright information follow]
prediction_gpt-5-mini__gpt-5-mini: Magnetic cassette tapes (analog magnetic tape) — they wear fastest with regular play because the tape physically contacts the heads, can shed oxide, stretch and demagnetize, and are highly sensitive to heat/humidity.

Using
DISINFECTING
WIPES
at home & school

Types of Antimicrobials

Sanitizers
Reduce bacterial numbers on surfaces to a safe level.

Disinfectants
Kill bacteria, fungi, and some viruses on surfaces.

Antiseptics/Drugs
Antiseptics like hand sanitizers treat or prevent diseases on living things. They are not pesticides.

Microbes
Bacteria
Fungi
Viruses
Spores*
*Most wipes do not kill spores

What is dwell time?
-The contact time (seconds or minutes) the product needs to stay on a surface to kill microbes
-The surface should be visibly wet for the entire dwell time
-Time may change based on the microbe and purpose (sanitize vs. disinfect)

Where can they be used?
-Specific locations listed only on the label
-Use sites and dwell time vary by location and purpose

Household surfaces
Medical
Public Health

What are they?
- Antimicrobials are intended to kill or slow the growth of microbes
-Disinfectant wipes are disposable for hard surfaces like counters and tables
-Surface cleaners like wipes are not intended for use on skin
- Disinfectant wipes may LOOK similar to hand wipes. Read the label! Look for phrases like "disinfecting", "antibacterial", or "kills germs, viruses, and bacteria". When in doubt, call NPIC! 800-858-7378

Use in schools and daycares
-Only adults should use cleaning wipes – children should NOT use wipes
-Keep out of reach of children
-Follow the dwell time listed on the label
-Leave wipes in the original container
-Use the right product for each situation
-Parents: ask how children are involved in classroom cleaning

These wipes are NOT for:
Food contact
Produce
Hand wipes
Baby wipes

What is antimicrobial resistance?
If similar products are used repeatedly, they may not be as effective over time. This resistance can occur naturally, but overuse or misuse of antimicrobial products increases the chances. Follow the label and dwell time to reduce the risk of resistance.

How do I minimize risk?
-Always follow the label, including use sites and dwell times
-Never mix antimicrobial or cleaning products
-You can wear gloves if you prefer, always check the label first
-Wash hands after use
-Avoid touching wet surfaces after use
-Follow first aid instructions
-Open windows or use fans
-Store wipes out of reach of children
-Removing dirt and food from surfaces before helps wipes work
-Misuse or overuse of wipes can lead to antimicrobial resistance

Where can I get more information?
npic.orst.edu
npic@ace.orst.edu
800.858.7378

THE BRUTAL ORIGINS

--- The tradition of Christmas is highly regarded as a celebration of the birth of Jesus Christ. Although popular belief states that Jesus was born on December 25th, The New Testament gives no specific date or year of his birth.

--- Roman pagans first introduced the holiday of "Saturnalia" which was a week-long period of lawlessness celebrated between December 17 - 25. During this period, Roman courts were closed, and Roman authorities stated that no one could be punished for breaking many of Rome's common laws.

--- The festival began when Roman authorities chose a random victim to represent "The Lord of Misrule." At the end of the festival on December 25th, the innocent man or woman would be brutally murdered. By doing so, Romans believed they were destroying the "forces of darkness".

--- In the 4th century C.E. Christianity adopted the "Saturnalia" festival in attempts to convert large amounts of people to Christianity. The problem was - there was nothing intrinsically Christian about the festival. extracted_text_claude_sonnet__claude_son:
Social Media Guidelines for Students and Job Seekers

Social media plays a big role in young people's lives. But how much of a role should social media play when it comes to educational or professional relationships?

Quick Stats:
- More than 75% of U.S. college students and young professionals check their Facebook page at least once a day.
- Most young professionals appear comfortable with connecting with colleagues on social networks.
- 36% of young employees aged 18-29 list an employer affiliation on Facebook.
- 16: The average number of colleagues young employees are friends with on Facebook.

Young Professionals in the U.S. Who Have Friended or Followed a Manager or Coworker:
- Neither colleagues or managers: 35%
- Only colleagues at work: 40%
- Only their manager at work: 4%
- Both managers and colleagues: 20%

Who Should You Friend?

Should young professionals and students add employers?
Make this decision carefully – find out if other coworkers are friends with your manager first.

For Students:
Classmates:
Pro:
- It's a great way to make friends and connect outside of class.
- You can connect with others for homework help.

Faculty:
Pro:
- Some faculty use social networks to expand on classroom discussions or to provide homework help.
- Friending a teacher could lead to better learning and educational opportunities.
Cons:
- Some students may not add you, but don't take this personally.
- Some view teacher-student interactions on social media as inappropriate.

For Professionals:
Coworkers:
Pro:
- Can help build inter-office relationships.
Cons:
- Connecting online may make balancing personal life and professional life difficult.
- May lead to gossip and other inter-office problems.

Competitors:
Pro:
- Friending competitors can show that you support and appreciate their accomplishments.
Cons:
- Your competitor may learn company secrets from you.
- Your current employer may think you want to leave.

Also consider:
- If you receive a friend request from a superior and want to accept, first clean up your profile and limit what he can see.
- You may want to wait on friending a superior until they decide to friend you first.
- If you receive a friend request from a superior and don't want to accept it, either ignore it or politely explain that you try to keep your personal and professional lives separate.
- Remember to never post workplace complaints online.
- Control who can see your posts and information by customizing privacy settings.
- Don't make it personal if someone decides not to friend you back.
- Don't friend coworkers or associates you don't trust.

Sources: cisco.com | moneyland.time.com | hrcommunication.com | mainstreet.com | education.com | kavahro.com | cbsnews.com | careerrookie.com | youtube.com | wikihow.com | huffingtonpost.com | abc2news.com
Information provided by: http://www.onlinedegrees.org/

HOW DOES A TOUCHSCREEN PHONE WORK?
TECHNOLOGY

There are three types of technologies widely used in touch screen phones today: resistive, capacitive, and infrared.

PHONES:
- Resistive: Touch Diamond, LG Dare
- Capacitive: Samsung Messenger Touch, Samsung Instinct, HTC Blackberry Storm
- Infrared (Heat and Optical): Samsung U800 (heat), Neonode N2 (optical)

HOW IT WORKS:
Resistive: When the top flexible layer is pushed into the lower layer, that location is calculated
Capacitive: The current is broadcast from the corners of the screen. When a finger touches the screen, a grid of invisible beams just above the screen detects that location.
Infrared: Heat-sensitive must be touched with a warm object to register point of touch.

COST TO MAKE:
Resistive: $
Capacitive: $$
Infrared: $$$

SCREEN MATERIAL:
Resistive: Layer of flexible material (usually polyester film) over a layer of glass
Capacitive: Glass
Infrared: Glass

TOOL FOR INPUT:
Resistive: Bare fingers, gloved fingers, or a stylus
Capacitive: Bare fingers only
Infrared: Heat-sensitive/warm, bare fingers or a stylus

OUTDOOR VISIBILITY:
Resistive: Poor visibility
Capacitive: Good visibility
Infrared: Good visibility, but strong ambient light impacts productivity

MULTI-TOUCH CAPABILITIES:
Resistive: No
Capacitive: Yes
Infrared: Yes

DURABILITY:
Resistive: Fair Durability, Screens are vulnerable to scratches and slow screen will wear over time and need replacing
Capacitive: Glass shatters upon major impact only
Infrared: Glass shatters upon major impact only

SOURCES:
http://www.phonescoop.com/articles/article.php?a=267&p=2690
http://www.macrho.com/td/GB188290

Key differentiating trait:
More of a niche platform, despite very large usage. Great for businesses that involve planning, design, creation, making. Heavily female audience.

Resource and expertise level needed: High
Good for these business types: Restaurants, Interior design, Wedding venues

Twitter
Key differentiating trait:
Fast-moving, real-time, news-driven platform. Strong customer service element, be prepared to engage and respond to customers here.
Best for: Link clicks, engagement, and foot traffic
Resource and expertise level needed: Low
Good for these business types: Retail, Nonprofits, Newspapers & media outlets

LinkedIn
Key differentiating trait:
Unlike almost every other platform, which is designed for the user to focus on their personal interests, this is a profession-driven platform.
Best for: Link clicks, event registrations
Resource and expertise level needed: Medium
Good for these business types: Luxury goods & services, Software providers, B2B companies

Instagram
Key differentiating trait:
There are very few ways to link from Instagram, so this is a brand-building platform. You need to have lots of high-quality images and videos to support this platform.
Best for: Foot traffic, word-of-mouth awareness, engagement
Resource and expertise level needed: Medium to high
Good for these business types: Bakeries & Coffee Shops, Travel agencies, Art museums

Facebook
Key differentiating trait:
This is the platform, but it doesn't mean you need to be there. With the recent challenges the platform has faced, some users are choosing to spend their social media time elsewhere (like Instagram, which is also a Facebook platform). That being said, there's still no better place to find and engage customers, donors, or others.
Best for: Link clicks, word-of-mouth awareness, event registrations
Resource and expertise level needed: Low
Good for these business types: Local Brick & Mortar Stores, Nonprofits, B2B Companies

1 Oct. 7, 2001: The first armed Predator drones begin flying missions over Afghanistan less than a month after 9/11. By March 2002, Drones would begin providing close air support for troops on the ground.

2 Nov. 3, 2002: A CIA-operated Predator drone launched a Hellfire missile at a car carrying five al-Qaida members. It was the first drone assassination in what would become a decadelong campaign.

3 Dec. 23, 2002: A Predator drone fires a sidewinder missile at an Iraqi fighter plane in the No Fly Zone. This is the first instance of air-to-air combat with a drone. During the surge in 2007, drone surveillance would significantly reduce the number of roadside bomb attacks.

4 May 7, 2004: The CIA begins a covert drone bombing campaign in Pakistan's tribal regions. Over the next four years, President George Bush would order 42 more drone strikes in the region. From 2008 to 2011, President Barack Obama has ordered at least 180 strikes. Some estimates put the death toll from the campaign at close to 2,000.

5 Late 2004: The U.S. Border Patrol begins testing UAVs for patrolling the U.S./Mexico border. In 2009, the U.S. begins flying drones inside of Mexican airspace in support of Mexican government antidrug cartel operations.

6 Late 2009: U.S. begins drone flights off Somalia to monitor and deter pirates. In June 2011, the U.S. begins basing drones at the Mogadishu airport and launching drone missile strikes within Somalia against al-Shabab militants linked to al-Qaida.

7 April 21, 2011: President Obama approves the use of drones in support of the Libyan rebels. The drones destroy Qaddafi air defenses in advance of the NATO bombing campaign, and provide targeting and support for NATO allies during the war. Operations in Libya also feature the first use of drone helicopters in combat.

8 Jan. 25, 2011: A Texas Department of Public Safety SWAT team deploys a small drone during a raid on a drug stash house. This marks the first use of UAVs for domestic surveillance.

The State Of The World's Female CEOs

How Many Women?
In 2010, (the most recent year of available data) only 12 Fortune Global 500 companies are run by women. This is down from 13 in 2009. [2.4%]

What about in America?:
The Standard & Poor's 500 index is made of: 484 male CEOs and 16 female CEOs
Females CEOs make up just 3.2% of America's S&P 500.
"Catalyst research studied Fortune 500 companies in the United States and found, on average, firms with the largest number of women senior executives compared with those with the fewest numbers, had a 35% higher return on equity."

Canada ahead of the rest:
In Canada, females hold 6.4% of all top officer positions at Financial Post 500* companies.
"Although there are 30 companies with women CEOs/heads, there are only 25 women who head those companies. [One female CEO] in particular heads four companies, [and another] heads two."
*(469 companies responding)

Top 3 Earning Women:
1. CEO of Yahoo! - $47.2 million
2. CEO of Kraft - $26.3 million
3. CEO of PepsiCo - $15.8 million

A Kellogg University School of Management study on woman leaders found that "as a board's gender composition evolved and increased its number of female seats, over time the likelihood that these boards would select female managers also increased."

A recent economic study published by the Wall Street Journal found that male CEOs with female daughters work to close the pay gap at their companies and pay female employees by as much as 13% in some cases. Birth of either male or female children to female CEOs produced no such effect.

Earning Statistics:
Average S&P 500 Salaries:
Male: Average $8 million
Female: Average $14 million
In 2009, male CEOs took a 5% cut, while salaries for female CEOs rose 19%.
For females this represents 43% more than their male counterparts.
S&P 500 aside, in a study of 1.1 million CEOs from companies of all sizes, revenues, etc, it was found that women CEOs only make about 75% of what male CEOs make.

Where do they come from?
"Women CEOs are almost twice as likely to have been named to the job from outside, as Cafaro was, than from within, according to a Harvard Business Review study."

Still a long way to go:
The highest paid male CEO is Gregory B. Maffei of Liberty Media Corp with a salary of $87.1 million. Compare this to [the highest paid female CEO] at just $47.2 million.
This means that Maffei is paid 184.5% more.

extracted_text_gpt-5-mini__gpt-5-mini:
EMPLOYEE SOCIAL MEDIA USAGE AT WORK
Statistics

How much of work time taken up per person in an average working day?

+120mins 9.4% +120mins
60-120 mins 9.6%
30-60 mins 12.3%
15-30 mins 17.2%
1-15 mins 18.7%
none 32.8%

This equates to a cost per employee per year in wasted wages of:

None 32.8% none
1-15 mins 18.7% up to £1,019.50
15-30 mins 17.2% up to £2,039
30-60 mins 12.3% up to £4,078
60-120 mins 9.6% up to £8,156
+120 mins 9.4% over £8,156

When employees use social media at work

40% intermittently throughout the day
11.6% during toilet breaks
4.5% other
41.4% during lunch
22.3% at the end of the day
6.4% when first arriving at the office

Why employees use social media at work

34% use social media while at work to take a mental break from their job
27% to connect with friends and family while at work
24% to make or support professional connections
17% to build or strengthen personal relationships with coworkers
20% to get information that helps them solve problems at work
17% to learn about someone they work with
12% to ask work-related questions of people outside their organization
12% to ask such questions of people inside their organization

Who is using it most:

25 mins women
39 mins men

Employers with and without social media policies

39% with
61% without

Impact of having a social media policy

Workers whose companies have an at-work social media policy say they use social media while on the job to:

30% take a break from work, compared with 40% of workers whose employers do not have such policies.
20% stay connected to family and friends while on the job, compared with 35% of workers whose social media use is not regulated at work.
Only 16% get information that's helpful to their job, compared with 25% of those whose workplaces have no such regulations

How employees view their use of social media

56% believe social media ultimately helps their job performance
22% believe that it mostly hurts
16% feel that it doesn't have much impact either way
6% see both the benefits and the drawbacks

The Cost of Employee Social Media

Averaged this is a cost of £2,592.60 per employee per year.

Multiplied by 30,645,000 employees in the UK, this is an annual cost to employers of £79,450,227,000 i.e. nearly £80 billion.

Factoring in lost productivity, this would cost even more.
8.13% of the working week is being lost on average. 8.13% of GDP is £185,884,320,000 i.e. nearly £186 billion in GDP lost every year to the UK.

Source/notes:

The cost per employee per year is based on a 2012 government report (NICE's Work-place Health report - Local government briefing [LG21]).
The NICE recommendations for local authorities and partner organisations on improving workplace health, using an average hourly rate of pay of £17.00 (£15.05 in 2012 increased by the increase in average weekly pay from 2012 to 2017), a national average week of 39.1 hours, a 5 day week and a 48 week working year.
30,645,000 people employed in the UK in 2016.
£2,286,400,000,000 GDP for UK in 2015.

Sources

http://www.callcentres.net/papers/employee-social-media-usage-at-work-marketing.pdf
http://www.somethingelse.com/social-media-in-the-workplace/ (additional source text as shown)

The explosion in digital data, bandwidth and processing power — combined with new tools for analyzing the data — has spurred massive interest in the emerging field of data science.

EDUCATION is the key to women's EMPOWERMENT

Who are the respondents?
1,006 Syrian women from inside and outside Syria participated in the survey.

Age Group Distribution

74% of respondents hold a university degree or higher.

What is the best way for women to get their rights?
92% of respondents believe that EDUCATION is the best way for women to obtain equal, or at least more, rights.

92% Educational Degree
7% Learn a Skill
1% Marriage

By educating women, we can empower them to give a voice to other women, and push for equal rights.

Do they believe Syrian women are empowered?
55% NO
42% YES

Graduate Degree
5.97%          3.19%

University Degree

2 year associate Degree
5.04%          4.94%

High School Degree
5.45%          5.25%

No high school degree
2.26%          1.75%

The results suggest the increased EDUCATION raises awareness about further opportunities for women's empowerment

Traditional schooling or Internet-based education?
5% Internet
17% Traditional
78% Both

Increasing access to Internet-based education can provide women in Syria with a significant opportunity.

Should educating women be stipulated and enforced?
100%

100% of respondents unanimously reported that education for women should be encouraged and enforced.

Do they believe that their daughters should go to school, no matter what?
0.3% NO
99.7% YES

Almost 100% of respondents believe that their daughter should go to school. Only 3 women disagreed.

EDUCATION is a powerful tool, and EVERYONE should be equipped with the appropriate tools to be able to shape their future into what they believe they deserve.

BAREEQ
Education & Development

Traditional TV and radio ads are being edged out by a younger, fresher digital option: influencer marketing.

Like

Influencer marketing pairs social media influencers, who have large trusted audiences on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram or Pinterest, with businesses looking to promote their products to new and existing customers through the influencer's channels.

This effective advertising option is taking off. Research shows 54% of companies have a stand-alone budget for sponsored social for their brand.

WHO THEY ARE

Social media icons with a great following and credibility within a certain niche. They could be:

YouTube Users
Industry Experts
Bloggers
Activists
Celebrities
Enthusiasts

WHAT THEY DO

Collaborate with businesses to create campaigns that increase brand awareness and drive sales by promoting a brand or product to their social audience.

CREATE          POST          INFLUENCE

Value-added content about a product or service to share with their blog readers and/or social channels

Product reviews, contests, giveaways, coupons, personal stories about products or services, videos, and more

Share content across multiple channels to increase awareness of the brand, product, or service

HOW INFLUENCERS MAKE MONEY

Businesses compensate influencers monetarily and/or with free samples of a product.

WHAT TO LOOK FOR IN AN INFLUENCER

HIGH ENGAGEMENT
You want influencers that are not only active online, but work to gain the trust of their followers

HIGH QUALITY CONTENT
An influencer should produce and share top-notch content, not run-of-the-mill articles that you can find anywhere on the Internet

MATCH YOUR BRAND
You want an influencer that matches your brand's tone, style and mission

Collaborate with businesses to create campaigns that increase brand awareness and drive sales by promoting a brand or product to their social audience

SPONSORED BLOG POST
A post written by an influencer to promote a business

SOCIAL SPONSORSHIP
Posts on an influencer's social channels to promote a business

INFLUENCER MARKETPLACE
Trusted source that connects businesses with influencers, & helps create a campaign

INFLUENCERS AGENCY
A company that helps businesses promote their brand through influencers

HOW TO CONNECT WITH INFLUENCERS

COVID-19
Coronavirus Disease 2019

BE AWARE.
COVID-19 is a new respiratory illness that was first discovered in Wuhan, China. It is transmitted from person to person.

COMMON SYMPTOMS:
- Runny nose
- Cough 
- Sore throat
- High fever
- Pneumonia
- Severe respiratory disease

SYMPTOMS*
*Symptoms may appear 1 to 12 days following exposure to the virus

Who is most at risk of becoming seriously ill?
- People over age 60
- People with pre-existing conditions such as diabetes and heart disease

How is it transmitted?
- Through close contact with an infected person
- By an infected person coughing or sneezing
- By touching contaminated objects or surfaces and then touching your mouth, nose or eyes

Currently there is no vaccine or specific treatment. We can only treat the symptoms.
Serious cases may require oxygen and ventilatory support.

PREPARE.
- Make sure you get your information from a reliable source.
- Wash your hands regularly with soap and water or use an alcohol-based gel.
- Avoid sharing cups, plates or other personal items and disinfect all surfaces that are touched frequently.
- If you have traveled to areas where COVID-19 is circulating or have been in contact with someone who has it and you experience fever, cough or difficulty breathing, seek medical attention immediately. Do not self-medicate.

ACT.
- Avoid close contact with people who have flu-like symptoms.
- Avoid touching your eyes, nose or mouth with unwashed hands.
- Cover your mouth with the inside of your elbow when you cough or sneeze or use a tissue and dispose of used tissue immediately and wash your hands.

Note: Information may change when more is known about the disease.
February 2020.

BE AWARE. PREPARE. ACT.
www.paho.org/coronavirus

PAHO Pan American Health Organization / World Health Organization

There are three types of technologies widely used in touch screen phones today: resistive, capacitive, and infrared.

- Capacitive: Apple's iPhone, HTC Hero, DROID Eris, Palm Pre
- Infrared: Samsung U800 (heat), Neonode N2 (optical)

He entered into a local cricket club called the Commando cricket club where he played from 1995–1998

His improved batting and keeping performances got him to be selected to the Bihar Ranji Trophy squad for the 1999/00 season

In the 1997/98 season, his superb performances in the Vinod Makna Under-16 Championship attracted the selector’s eye

He made his debut against the Assam team by scoring an unbeaten score of 68

In 2003, he was selected to the India A team which went to tour Kenya to play a tri-series tournament with Pakistan as the third team

One-Day Internationals

He was run out for a duck but in his 5th ODI match against Pakistan, he scored 148 off 123 balls to be called a match winning player in the Indian squad

In the series he scored 362 runs at an average of 72.40 with 3 back to back centuries against Pakistan

One-Day Internationals

He won the ‘Man of the Series’ award for top scoring in the tournament with 346 runs

He was made the captain of the Indian T20 squad in September 2007

He was made the captain of the ODI team in 2007 and the Test team in 2008

He immediately made impact as he led the team to win the inaugural World Cup T20 in South Africa

In 2009, he scored 1198 runs in 24 innings, an average of 70.43 to be the highest scorer of the year in ODI’s

Only a few players have been wicket- keepers Captains for their team for a long time. Dhoni ranks at no.1 beating Gary Alexander by over 15 who ranks second in that list

highest scoring wicket-keeper

He’s not just a batsman and a wicket keeper, but our Captain has proven himself as a bowler too. He took the wicket of Travis Dowlin of West Indies

He is the highest scoring wicket-keeper captain on the world in a Test. He’s the only wicket keeper in the world who has taken a wicket

He has been awarded the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna which is the highest sports honor given by the government of India in the year 2008

He advertises more than 15 products as of 2013 and is one of the most marketable players in the world

Dhoni is the successful captain that India ever had. He led India to 27 Test match victories in 60 Tests and 107 ODI wins in 194 ODIs as a captain

Win 40 T20 matches

He captained 70 T20 matches and he had 40 wins in his account

In Indian Premier League, he signed up with Chennai Super Kings in 2008, and in the last six seasons, has won the trophy twice. He’s the only captain to have achieved that feat

In February of 2015, the couple welcomed a baby girl named Ziva

In 2010, Mahi married Sakshi, his childhood sweetheart

2016 IPL

Now Dhoni Captaining Rising Pune Supergiants in 2016 IPL

On 4th January, 2017, Dhoni stepped down as captain of India’s limited overs teams ahead of the ODIs and T20s against England, on 6th Jan 2017 Virat Kohli became the new captain

He will definitely be remembered as one of the most successful Indian captains in the history of the sport

He took the decision in order to give the younger players a chance to lead the side to the next level

‘M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story’ is a Bollywood biographical film directed by Neeraj Pandey, starring by Sushant Singh Rajput based on the life of Mahendra Singh Dhoni

The estimate earning of MS Dhoni is approximately Rs. 25 crore per annum

As Team USA is preparing for the Olympic Winter Games 2018, it is important to realize how The Games have changed over the years for Team USA. ENHANCED TRAINING WITH TECHNOLOGY FOR TEAM USA

1924
Early Days for Team USA at the Olympic Winter Games

Inability to monitor vitals or receive feedback during training

Could not learn from the practice runs, which made coaching a guessing game

Difficult to access medical history and past injuries slowed down recovery times

NOW
Technology Helps Prevent Injuries and Speeds Recovery for Team USA

Skin patches monitor heart rate, body temperature and sweat composition during training

Coaches learn from live to last from a mobile app

Quick access to past medical records shortens recovery time

TEAM USA ATHLETES PRACTICE SMARTER, NOT HARDER

1936
First Alpine Skiing Event for Team USA

Trained the old-fashioned way — skiing down mountains

Warm weather locations at a disadvantage, couldn't train year-round

No feel for the actual course beforehand

NOW
Team USA Can Now Simulate Ski Runs

Immersive video technology Team USA skiers emulate any course

Athletes get to know the course without leaving the training facility

Immediate feedback allows for improvement

13 hours of coverage over 11 days

15-minute selection of taped highlights aired from 11:15-11:30PM

NOW
Broadcast TV Not the Only Way to Watch Team USA

Today's smartphones allow you to watch video in high definition

Only 9% of Americans reported owning smartphones in 2008

Nearly impossible to have coverage of The Games while on-the-go

In The Palm of Everyone's Hand

Now 77% of Americans own smartphones!

Livestream any Team USA event from any mobile device

Gain extra insight into the Team USA athletes

HOW TEENS VIEW THEIR DIGITAL LIVES

Ninety percent of America's teens have used social media, and the majority of them believe it's a more positive than negative influence. But many say they're "addicted" to their devices and that they want to disconnect sometimes. Teens also say that talking in person is better than texting, and face-to-face conversations are more fun than communicating online. Teens tell us:

51%

68% [Facebook icon]
6% [Twitter icon]
1% [Google+ icon]

23% OF US USE AT LEAST TWO DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOCIAL MEDIA EVERYDAY

SOCIAL NETWORKING MAKES US FEEL BETTER THAN WORSE

We feel that social networking helps our relationships with friends (52%) and family (37%) vs. hurts our relationships with friends (4%) and family (2%).

And it makes us feel less shy (29%) and more confident (20%) vs. more shy (3%) and less confident (4%).

Only 5% of us believe that social networking makes us feel more depressed vs. 10% who feel less depressed.

WE PREFER FACE TIME TO SCREEN TIME

Talking in person is our favorite way to communicate because it's more fun and easier to understand what other people mean. If we can't do that, we text. Very few of us prefer social networking as our go-to way to connect, and many of us believe that using social media can interfere with face-to-face communication.

TALK TO ME!
Our favorite way to communicate is:
IN PERSON (49%)
BY TEXT (33%)
ON SOCIAL NETWORKS (7%)
BY PHONE (4%)
ON TWITTER (1%)

"Moments only happen in person"
"I enjoy seeing [my friends] laugh at my jokes."
"It's boring to talk to someone that has to check Facebook every 5 minutes."

WE HAVE A BAD CONNECTION...
Many of us believe that using social media:
Takes away from time we can spend with others face to face (34%)
Distracts us from the people we're with (44%)
Creates frustration when our friends pay more attention to their gadgets than to us (45%)

SOMETIMES WE GET TIRED OF BEING WIRED

Many of us sometimes wish that we — and the people around us — would disconnect. It's not just about being tired of technology; it turns out that many of us who talk about tuning out have encountered some derogatory digital dialogue.

36% of us sometimes wish we could go back to a time when there was no Facebook.

More than a third of us who want to unplug often encounter racist, sexist, and homophobic content.

41% of us consider ourselves "addicted" to our phones, and 20% to social sites. 43% of us sometimes wish we could "unplug", and 21% wish our parents would, too.

"Sometimes it's nice to just sit back and relax with no way possible to communicate with anyone in any way. That's why I occasionally 'lose' my cell phone."

"Technology and social networking have absorbed some of my close friendships, and they've dwindled. I miss the times where we could just go hang out and laugh."

SOCIAL MEDIA, SOCIAL LIFE: IT'S NOT GENDER NEUTRAL

Every day, more teen girls than teen boys text. In the daily Twitter-verse, there are also more girls than boys, and, when it comes to photo posting, it's especially a girl thing...but not always a good thing.

77% TEXT DAILY
33% EVER TWEETED
75% LOVE POSTING PHOTOS

60% TEXT DAILY
22% EVER TWEETED
42% LOVE POSTING PHOTOS

57% girls vs. 28% boys sometimes feel left out after seeing photos of others together online
45% girls vs. 24% boys worry about other people posting ugly photos of them online
28% girls vs. 9% boys have edited photos of themselves before posting

extracted_text_gpt-5-nano__gpt-5-nano:
7 SCIENCE-BACKED WAYS READING MAKES YOU HEALTHY

Increase longevity by 237

Study:
A 2016 study observed the reading habits of 3655 people. The participants were classified into three groups:
- Read books 3.5 hours or more per week
- Read books 1–3.5 hours per week
- Did not read at all.

The result:
When followed up over 12 years, it was found that those who read more books lived longer.
- Read books for more than 3.5 hours a week were 23% less likely to die than those who did not read books.
- Read books for more than 3.5 hours a week were 17% less likely to die when compared to those who did not read books.

Why book readers live longer?
Reading books provides a higher survival advantage than reading newspapers or magazines because book readers undergo deep reading.

This means readers:
- Draw connections between the parts of the material
- Find applications to the outside world
- Ask questions about the content

Deep reading aids survival because:
- Engaging the brain throughout your life reduces cognitive decline
- A good social network has been shown to be associated with a higher survival rate
- Friends can provide support and a source of comfort, shielding from the harmful impacts of stress
- Friends can also bring purpose and meaning to people’s lives

Reduces stress
2 REDUCES STRESS
Reading reduces stress levels by 68% more than any other activity

Reading 68%
Listening to music 61%
Drinking tea or coffee 54%
Walking 42%

Just 6 minutes of reading:
- Slows your heart rate
- Eases muscle tension
- Alters your state of mind

Promotes relaxation and sleep
3 PROMOTES RELAXATION AND SLEEP
Reading can help you sleep if you make it a part of your nightly routine.

Why?
- It helps relax your body and mind
- It helps your mind settle
- It can provide a comforting routine

Make sure it’s a printed book

Slows off Alzheimer’s disease, dementia and mental decline
4 SLOWS OFF ALZHEIMER’S DISEASE, DEMENTIA AND MENTAL DECLINE
Readers are 2.5 times less likely to develop Alzheimer’s disease.
Frequent brain exercises, like reading, decreases mental decline in the elderly by 32%.

Why?
Reading is mentally stimulating and helps keep the brain active.
All the developments in the brain are supported by reading and mental activity

Helps with depression
5 HELPS WITH DEPRESSION
Reading can be an important non-medical intervention for people with depression.
Reading counts, conducted at a mental health drop-in center and at a GP surgery, reduce symptoms of depression in participants.

The participants in the reading group reported feeling more:
- Confident
- Talkative
- Willing to listen
- Willing to interact with the other group participants

Boosts happiness
6 BOOSTS HAPPINESS
Life experiences you gain through reading give you happiness that material things cannot always provide.
Other experiential products include:
- Sporting goods
- Video games
- Musical instruments

Reading can also help fill up your fulfillment tank.
A study found that older adults who continue to read and learn are more satisfied with their lives.
Another study has shown that those who read books reported better social connections and more happiness with their lives.

Increases social connection
7 INCREASES SOCIAL CONNECTION
Reading develops theory of mind, which leads to better social connection.
Social connections increase life expectancy.
A meta-analysis shows that across 148 studies, social connections are linked to longer life.
Loneliness and social isolation have been shown to be associated with early death.

GLOBAL English Editing
For a more detailed analysis of the benefits of reading, please visit geeting7-science-backed-ways-reading-makes-you-healthy-infographic

HOW SOCIAL MEDIA CHANGED THE AD GAME

Almost every industry has been disrupted by the rise of social media. Nowhere is that more plain than in the world of advertising. To keep up with consumer and technological trends, here's a look at some of these key changes.

CUSTOMERS
Today, social media makes up the largest portion of how consumers spend their time online. Marketers have had to adjust online expressions to target consumers where they devote most of their attention.

TOP ONLINE CATEGORIES by Total Internet Time:
- Social Network/Blogs: 22.5%
- Online Games: 9.8%
- Email: 7.6%
- Videos/Movies: 4.5%
- Search: 4%
- Instant Messaging: 3.3%
- Software Manufacturers: 3.2%
- Classifieds/Auctions: 2.9%
- Current Events/Global News: 2.6%
- "Other": 35.1%
*"Other" includes multi-category entertainment, adult, Internet tools/web services, mass merchants, auction, corporate information, sports, government, news/info, and more.

PRIORITIES
Even several years into the era of Web 2.0, advertisers are still seeing increased demand for online marketing services. At the same time, nearly all other ad media are losing priority.

Ad Media Clients Are Most Focused on According to U.S. Ad Agencies:
[Charts showing Q1-Q3 2011 data for Spot TV, Internet/Digital, Spot Radio, Out of Home]

SPENDING
Thanks to clients' changing priorities and the ever-evolving social media landscape, CMOs at some of the top Fortune 500 companies forecast that spending will likely double current levels in the next five years.

Social Marketing Budget Changes from 2010-2011:
- Increased: 45%
- Stayed the Same: 36%
- Decreased: 1%
- Don't Know: 13%

Planned Social Media Spending as Share of Marketing Budget According to CMOs:
Current: 7.1%
In the Next 12 Months: 10.1%
In the Next 5 Years: 17.5%

TACTICS
Old ad models aren't sustainable on new media. With a new set of popular platforms, traditional advertisers have had to adopt new tactics.

MOST POPULAR TACTICS FOR ENGAGING SOCIAL FOLLOWERS:
1. Social share buttons on brand website
2. Unique content exclusively for fans/followers 
3. "Like" buttons on brand website
4. Brand videos on YouTube, Vimeo, etc.
5. Monitoring social media for brand reputation

METRICS
Generating leads and boosting sales are no longer the top aim of marketers. Social media offers a slew of new metrics to measure marketing success.

Ways Marketers Measure Social Media Marketing Success:
- Numbers linking as friends, followers, likes: 60%
- Qualified leads from social media: 39%
- Sharing, forwarding, retweeting, or posting brand content: 35%
- Visits or time spent with branded social content: 30%
- Brand awareness/favorability: 25%
- Incremental sales attributable to social media: 18%

SOURCES: EXPERIAN, STRATA, CHIEF MARKETER, AMERICAN MARKETING ASSOCIATION, DUKE FUQUA SCHOOL OF BUSINESS

The idea of a shared universe — multiple films and characters that cross over into one another — has been central to Marvel Studios' approach since Iron Man (2008). DC Extended Universe
